title: JavaScript Control Structures Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of if...else and switch statements in JavaScript.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a falsy value in JavaScript?
What is the purpose of the 'break' statement in a switch case?
options: To prevent execution from falling through to the next case
To return a value from the switch

questions.question: What does 'else if' represent?
options: The first condition to be checked
The default condition if all else fails
An intermediate condition checked if the previous 'if' was false

questions.question: In a switch statement, what is the 'default' case used for?
options: The code that runs if no case matches the expression

questions.question: Which and/or what is the inherent boolean value of the string 'false' in JavaScript?
